question_text: A light bulb is switched on for 30 seconds and transfers 6000 joules of energy. Calculate the power of the light bulb.
explanation: Power is calculated using the formula: Power = Energy ÷ Time. In this case, Power = 6000 J ÷ 30 s = 200 W. This is a straightforward one-step calculation where students must apply the power formula to find the rate of energy transfer.
